steps.title: Set up the mean formula for frequency distributions
steps.content:
The mean of a frequency distribution is:
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{\sum(\text{value} \times \text{frequency})}{\sum \text{frequency}}$$

All four choices contain the same dollar values (\$70, \$80, \$90, \$100). Since values are identical, the highest mean depends on which distribution weights values toward the higher end.

steps.title: Calculate the mean for each choice
steps.content:
Choice B (equal frequencies 6, 6, 6, 6):
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{70(6) + 80(6) + 90(6) + 100(6)}{24} = \frac{2040}{24} = 85$$

Choice A (increasing frequencies 4, 5, 6, 7):
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{70(4) + 80(5) + 90(6) + 100(7)}{22} = \frac{1920}{22} \approx 87.27$$

Choice C (symmetric frequencies 7, 6, 6, 7):
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{70(7) + 80(6) + 90(6) + 100(7)}{26} = \frac{2210}{26} = 85$$

Choice D (symmetric frequencies 8, 5, 5, 8):
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{70(8) + 80(5) + 90(5) + 100(8)}{26} = \frac{2210}{26} = 85$$

Choice A's increasing frequency distribution progressively weights more data on the higher values (\$90 and \$100), producing a mean of 87.27—the greatest among all choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
